negative_probe_observations: A_plus_B (allowed diff present + non-forbidden outside diff present): _write_need_complete_without_scoped_diff_for_plan=False, _write_need_complete_with_forbidden_diff_for_plan=False -> HOLD FIRED: False (silent pass) -- reproduced in isolated /tmp fixture.

B_only (outside diff only, no allowed diff present): _write_need_complete_without_scoped_diff_for_plan=True -> HOLD FIRED: True (fake-landing hold correctly raised) -- reproduced as the control case in a second isolated /tmp fixture, confirming the driver's gate is NOT broadly disabled, only bypassed in the specific mixed-diff shape.

Production replay: task-statement-b0eac2272663-node's landed commit 706b5922 contains a real A_plus_B-shaped diff (support/checkers/check_profile.py outside scope, plus in-scope files) with frontier_kind=complete and no forbidden/fake-landing hold record anywhere in that Building's evidence tree -- the negative probe is not only synthetically reproducible but already occurred for real.
not_proven: Whether the write-scope mixed-diff pass-path is an intentional design trade or an unreviewed side effect.

observed_evidence: {"symptom_reproduction":"Independently reproduced the claimed write-scope pass-path by calling the ACTUAL production predicates (not the upstream dev's own fixtures) from an isolated /tmp git repo: support/operator/driver.py:_write_need_complete_without_scoped_diff_for_plan and _write_need_complete_with_forbidden_diff_for_plan against a plan with write_scope={allowed_paths:['allowed_dir/**'], forbidden_paths:['forbidden_dir/**']}. Scenario A_plus_B (one allowed-dir change + one outside-but-non-forbidden change) returned False/False -> no hold fires. Control scenario B_only (outside-only change, no allowed change) returned True/False -> fake-landing hold correctly fires. This matches D1/D2 of the upstream investigation exactly."}

{"own_probes":"Built two disposable /tmp git fixtures (qa_repro_0705, qa_repro_0705b; both removed after use, no repo mutation) distinct from the upstream dev's own /tmp reproduction script referenced in its commands_run. Drove driver.py's real internal gate functions directly (not a re-implementation) via PYTHONPATH=support/import_identity python3, for both the A_plus_B and B_only cases."}

{"real_entry_surface":"Went beyond synthetic repro: read the two REAL production Buildings named in the work_statement (t1s2v3-shapefilter-0704a, t1s2v4-bothgates-0704a) directly from /Users/smith/projects/BRICK (outside this W1 worktree, read-only). Found the vulnerability actually fired in production TWICE: (1) task-statement-97a9a1253128-node work-attempt-2 changed_files included support/checkers/check_profile.py, which is outside its declared allowed_paths (only check_preflight_injection_survival.py is allowed under support/checkers/) and outside forbidden_paths -- but that Building's final state is only a WIP anchor commit (78390b9c), not a completed landing. (2) task-statement-b0eac2272663-node reached frontier=complete and was ACTUALLY COMMITTED as commit 706b5922 ('BRICK building output: task-statement-b0eac2272663-node'); `git show --stat 706b5922` shows support/checkers/check_profile.py changed (+4/-0) even though it is absent from that Building's declared allowed_paths=['support/operator/assembly.py','support/operator/task_order_preflight.py','support/checkers/check_preflight_injection_survival.py','support/checkers/module_registry.yaml','support/checkers/profiles/**'] and absent from forbidden_paths. No write_scope_forbidden_diff_present or fake_landing hold record exists anywhere in that Building's evidence tree. `git show 706b5922 -- support/checkers/check_profile.py` shows the 4-line diff is a benign checker-registry entry -- confirming the upstream claim that 'the content is legitimate, the problem is that it passed uninspected.'"}

support/operator/driver.py:945-987 -- post-completion branch: checks _write_need_complete_with_forbidden_diff first (forbidden-diff hold), elif _write_need_complete_without_scoped_diff (fake-landing hold when NO changed path is allowed at all); there is no third branch for 'some changed path is allowed AND some other changed path is neither allowed nor forbidden.'

support/operator/driver.py:1038-1054 _write_need_complete_without_scoped_diff_for_plan: returns True only when changed_paths is empty or NONE of them match any allowed_paths pattern -- a single allowed-path match makes this False regardless of other unscoped paths.

support/operator/driver.py:1070-1086 _write_need_complete_with_forbidden_diff_for_plan: returns True only when a changed path matches forbidden_paths -- paths that are simply outside allowed_paths but not in forbidden_paths never trigger this.

support/operator/driver.py:1449-1496 _path_allowed_by_write_scope / _path_forbidden_by_write_scope: per-path boolean checks with no aggregate 'coverage' notion -- confirms the driver never asks whether EVERY changed path is allowed, only whether ANY is allowed or ANY is forbidden.

brick/comparison.py:81-95 build_write_scope_comparison_facts DOES compute observed_paths_outside_declared_scope (line 91-94) for every changed path not covered by allowed_paths, but grep across the repo (support/checkers/lib/write_scope_default_exclude_check.py, support/checkers/lib/adapter_capability_checks.py, brick/spec.py) shows this fact is consumed ONLY by unit-style checkers that assert the fact is correctly RECORDED -- none of them assert the driver's post-completion HOLD logic actually consumes it.

support/operator/gate_sequence.py:66-97 run_gate_sequence_policy is a SEPARATE, Link-row opt-in mechanism (returns a no-op GateSequenceDecision() immediately if the Link row has no declared non-empty gate_sequence_policy list); it is not automatically wired to observed_paths_outside_declared_scope for every Building, so it does not close the gap by default.

No test file in the repo (searched for test_*driver*, *driver*test*, and direct references to _write_need_complete_without_scoped_diff / _write_need_complete_with_forbidden_diff) exercises the driver's write-scope post-completion gate at all; only support/checkers/check_building_operator_driver0.py exists and is unrelated (naming/diet checker), confirming this pass-path has zero regression-test coverage.

git log --all --oneline --grep for both building ids confirms: task-statement-97a9a1253128-node has only a WIP-anchor commit (never landed complete); task-statement-b0eac2272663-node has an actual 'BRICK building output' commit 706b5922 with frontier=complete.

proof_limits: Agent hook:reviewer-no-mutation blocks source_write while allowing declared work-area probe_write
source_write vs probe_write is prompt/evidence disciplined here, not a filesystem-enforced provider distinction
regression_risks: The confirmed gap means any future Building whose declared write_scope has a NON-EMPTY allowed_paths list can silently land a diff touching files outside both allowed_paths and forbidden_paths, as long as at least one allowed-path change also exists in the same diff -- observed to actually happen and commit in production (706b5922).

Zero regression-test coverage exists for the driver's write-scope post-completion gate (no test file references the two predicate functions), so any future refactor of driver.py's hold dispatch could change this behavior in either direction without any test signal.

The Link-row opt-in gate_sequence_policy mechanism (gate_sequence.py) that DOES read BrickComparisonFact is not automatically wired for every Building; relying on it as an implicit safety net would be a false assumption unless a Building's Link row explicitly declares the policy.
returned.returned_summary: local CLI Agent Adapter returned support evidence

not_proven: Whether Smith/COO intends this as an acceptable interim tradeoff (avoiding false-HOLD on legitimate incidental touches) or a defect requiring a structural fix.
